_comment: Single source of truth for the recipe catalog. Consumed by the dashboard (Vite JSON import in dashboard/src/lib/recipes.js) and by the proxy (include_str! in src/recipes.rs, served to agents via /agent/services + /agent/recipes). The CLI catalog (tap-cli/src/recipe.rs) is a known duplicate — folding it onto this file is a follow-up.

recipes.title: Invoice payer
recipes.pitch: Scan your email for invoices and pay them from your bank.

creds.name: gmail
creds.label: Gmail (read-only)
creds.note: Reads your inbox to find invoices — it can never send or delete.

creds.name: mercury
creds.label: Mercury
creds.note: Pays approved invoices — every payment needs your passkey.

recipes.taskPrompt: You are an invoice assistant with TAP access to Gmail (read) and Mercury (payments). Search Gmail for unpaid invoices, extract the amount, payee, and due date for each, show me a summary, and — once I confirm — pay each via Mercury. Every payment is gated behind my passkey approval, so send them through TAP and wait for me to approve.

recipes.title: Social ghostwriter
recipes.pitch: Drafts and publishes social posts — nothing goes out without your tap.

flavors.label: Mastodon

creds.name: mastodon
creds.label: Mastodon
creds.keyHint: Preferences → Development → New application (on another instance, edit the allowed host after)
creds.note: Reads your feed instantly; every status it posts waits for your tap.

flavors.taskPrompt: You are my social ghostwriter with TAP access to Mastodon (credential 'mastodon'). Read my notifications and recent posts — reads are instant — then draft replies and posts. Publishing is the chokepoint: every status POST goes through TAP approval showing the exact text, so publish one at a time and wait for my tap before the next.

flavors.label: Discord

creds.name: discord-bot
creds.label: Discord bot
creds.keyHint: Developer portal → your app → Bot → Token
creds.note: Reads your channels instantly; every message it sends waits for your tap.

flavors.taskPrompt: You are my community announcer with TAP access to a Discord bot (credential 'discord-bot'). Read the channels the bot can see to catch up — reads are instant — and draft the announcements or replies I ask for. Every message send goes through TAP approval showing the exact text, so post one at a time and wait for my tap before the next.

flavors.label: X / Twitter

creds.name: twitter
creds.label: X / Twitter
creds.keyHint: Developer portal → your app → Keys and tokens
creds.note: Reads mentions instantly; every tweet it posts waits for your tap.

flavors.taskPrompt: You are my social ghostwriter with TAP access to X/Twitter (credential 'twitter'). Read my mentions and timeline — reads are instant — then draft replies and tweets. Publishing is the chokepoint: every tweet POST goes through TAP approval showing the exact text, so publish one at a time and wait for my tap before the next.

recipes.title: PR & CI copilot
recipes.pitch: Triages your PRs and CI; repo actions only run on your approval.

creds.name: github
creds.label: GitHub
creds.keyHint: Fine-grained token with Pull requests + Actions access
creds.note: Triage reads are instant; re-runs, merges, and comments wait for your tap.

recipes.taskPrompt: You are my repo copilot with TAP access to the GitHub API (credential 'github'). Triage my repos: list open PRs, failing checks, and reviews waiting on me — reads are instant — and give me a "what's blocking" summary. When I ask you to act — re-run a failed workflow, merge an approved green PR, post a comment — each write goes through TAP approval, so fire it and wait for my tap before the next action.

recipes.title: Support refund agent
recipes.pitch: Reviews Stripe disputes and refunds customers — passkey on every refund.

creds.name: stripe
creds.label: Stripe
creds.keyHint: A test-mode key works for trying it
creds.note: Reads charges and disputes instantly — every refund needs your passkey.

recipes.taskPrompt: You are my support assistant with TAP access to Stripe (credential 'stripe'). Review recent charges and disputes — reads are instant — and flag which refund requests look legitimate, with your reasoning. Once I agree, process each refund via TAP: refunds are passkey-gated, so send the refund POST, then wait for me to approve it with my passkey. One refund at a time, never batch.

recipes.title: Meeting scheduler
recipes.pitch: Negotiates meeting times over email and manages your calendar itself.

creds.name: google-scheduler
creds.label: Google (Gmail + Calendar)
creds.note: Emails invitees (every send waits for your tap) and manages events on its own.

recipes.taskPrompt: You are my scheduling assistant with TAP access to Google (credential 'google-scheduler': Gmail + Calendar). To schedule a meeting: check my calendar for free slots — reads are instant — then email the invitee two or three options. Every outgoing email is approval-gated, so send it via TAP and wait for my tap. Read their reply, and once a time is agreed, create the calendar event yourself with sendUpdates=none (event writes on my own calendar are auto-approved), then send a short confirmation email (gated, like every email). Never send an email without my approval; the calendar is yours to manage.
